text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town nestled between two large rivers, there lived a gentle owl named Oliver Owlwell.

Each day after sundown, Oliver would venture out into the town to explore. One evening, as he flew along the birdpole at the candlelit market, he caught sight of something unusual—an old, rusty bell hanging from a gnarled tree branch. He fluttered over and discovered it was a bell tolling softly. He had never heard it before.

Curiosity got the better of him. He hopped right down onto the tree branch and rang it softly. There was no response. Once again, he rang it. The woodcutter next door was heard humming a cheerful tune. Oliver flew closer, then tapped the bell again. There was a tiny bell-shaped hole next to the rim.

His curiosity was piqued. He inched closer. Inside was a small, tiny cricket. Oliver couldn't believe his eyes—Illusionist Merlin had made a
